analysis.reasoning: Core message: A U.S. official praises meeting Georgia’s president, stresses paramount U.S.–Georgia cooperation, and declares unwavering U.S. support for Georgia’s sovereignty against Russian occupation. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not explicitly about U.S. historical values or legacy; no. US2 (admirable society) – No reference to U.S. society, groups, or heroes; no. US3 (hostile enemies) – While Russia is described as occupying Georgia, the focus is not on threats to the U.S. itself; the frame is support for Georgia rather than emphasizing enemies of the U.S.; no. US4 (force for good) – Yes, it highlights U.S. support for sovereignty and opposition to occupation, aligning with promoting freedom and security. US5 (great international ally) – Yes, it emphasizes unwavering U.S. support and cooperation with an ally (Georgia). US6 (at the forefront) – No positioning of U.S. leadership or superiority; no. Conclusion: US4 and US5 apply.
